key_events_next_24h.note: First Friday of May. Strong jobs = less Fed cut pressure = bearish for BTC. Weak = dovish pivot hope = bullish. Key binary catalyst within 24h.

analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$76,000-$76,350 on Apr 30 (20:00 UTC). Down ~2% in 24h. Twice rejected at $80K resistance (double top). Support ~$74,900. Progressive lower opens each session this week.
sentiment_key_signals: FOMC Apr 28-29: held 3.5-3.75%, hawkish 8-4 dissent — most divided since Oct 1992
Still within post-FOMC 48h window — historical BTC weakness pattern active
BTC twice rejected at $80K — firm technical ceiling (double top pattern)
Spot BTC ETF: -$137.77M on Apr 29 (third confirmed consecutive outflow day)
30-year US Treasury yield at ~5.0% — highest since Jul 2025; risk-off capital rotation
US Q1 GDP 2026: 2.0% advance estimate — missed 2.2% forecast
NFP May 1 at 12:30 UTC — next high-impact binary catalyst within 24h
Geopolitical: Iran / Strait of Hormuz tensions, oil elevated, risk-off sentiment
Global crypto market cap $2.63T, down ~0.4% over 24h; total volume $107.3B
analysis.macro_backdrop: Post-FOMC hawkish hold (3.5-3.75%, 8-4 split against further cuts). 30yr yield at 5% signals sustained inflation concern and rate-cut delay. Q1 GDP missed at 2.0% vs 2.2%. ETF outflows three consecutive days. BTC double-topped at $80K. NFP May 1 is next major catalyst — bearish lean maintained until data confirms direction.
analysis.regime_rationale: Bearish (0.65): FOMC hawkish aftermath, $80K double rejection, consecutive ETF outflows, 30yr yield at 5%, GDP miss, Fear & Greed 33. Confidence 0.65 (>=0.5 threshold) — macro filter ACTIVE. Prefer SHORT bias or cautious HOLD until NFP May 1 12:30 UTC resolves.
